summary: Implementation plan for the 1985-NY-Yankees Azure Function: a nightly Timer-triggered Python 3.11 function that queries GPT-4o via TRAPI, validates the roster response, and persists it to a dedicated Azure Blob Storage account — all authenticated via Managed Identity. Covers IaC provisioning, application code, CI/CD, security, observability, and release readiness.
assumptions: TRAPI supports Azure AD Managed Identity bearer token authentication; if not, a Key Vault-stored API key fallback will be used.
TRAPI endpoint URL and GPT-4o model version will be confirmed before APP-01 begins.
Roster scope is the 25-man active roster only (coaching staff excluded) until confirmed otherwise.
TRAPI is accessible over public internet; no VNet integration required for initial deployment.
Consumption Plan is acceptable; strict cold-start SLA is not required for the 2 AM execution window.
Existing infra/ Bicep modules (storage, functionapp, rbac, monitoring, alerts) will be extended rather than replaced.
GitHub Actions is already configured for the repository; a new workflow for this function will be added.
Python 3.11 and azure-functions v2 SDK are the target runtime as stated in spec.
The dedicated storage account is separate from the Function App host storage account.
Application Insights workspace-based instance will be reused or provisioned via the existing monitoring Bicep module.
open_questions: Does TRAPI support Azure AD Managed Identity bearer tokens? If not, what credential type and Key Vault secret name will be used?
What is the TRAPI base URL and GPT-4o model version (pin value) to use in the prompt template?
Should the roster include only the 25-man active roster, or also coaching staff, injured list, and front-office personnel?
Is TRAPI network-restricted (requiring VNet integration and private endpoint for storage)?
Is there a strict SLA on the 2 AM execution time that would require a Premium Plan with always-ready instances?

waves.name: Foundation
waves.objective: Establish all infrastructure, environment configuration, secret management, and CI/CD scaffolding required before any application code is written.
entry_criteria: TRAPI endpoint and auth mechanism confirmed by owner
Azure subscription and resource group available
GitHub repository access and Actions permissions granted
exit_criteria: All Bicep modules deployed to staging environment without errors
Managed Identity role assignments verified via Azure portal or CLI
Key Vault provisioned and TRAPI credentials (if needed) stored as secrets
GitHub Actions workflow scaffold triggers successfully on push to main
Implementation task plan reviewed and approved

waves.name: Core Build
waves.objective: Implement the core application logic: TRAPI client, prompt template, response validation, blob writer, and Timer Trigger function wiring.
entry_criteria: TRAPI endpoint and model version confirmed
Local dev environment configured with local.settings.json
exit_criteria: Function executes end-to-end locally against TRAPI staging
Validated roster blob written to dedicated storage account
Failed-path blob written to failed/ prefix on validation failure
All unit tests passing with >80% coverage on core modules

waves.name: Hardening
waves.objective: Add reliability controls, structured observability, security hardening, and data quality alerting to production-ready standards.
entry_criteria: Core Build wave exit criteria met
exit_criteria: Retry/backoff logic verified under simulated TRAPI failure
All three alert rules active in Application Insights (failure, duration, data quality)
Structured log events emitted and queryable in Log Analytics
Conditional PUT idempotency verified on same-day retrigger

waves.name: Release Readiness
waves.objective: Validate end-to-end in staging, execute slot swap to production, confirm monitoring is live, and complete documentation.
exit_criteria: Production deployment completed via GitHub Actions slot swap
Nightly schedule verified with at least one successful production execution
All success criteria from spec met

tasks.title: Review and approve implementation task plan
tasks.description: Review this task plan with stakeholders, confirm TRAPI auth mechanism and endpoint, resolve open questions on player scope and networking, and formally approve before implementation begins.
outputs: Resolved open questions documented in ADR or spec update
acceptance_criteria: All five open questions from spec have a recorded decision (even if deferred)
TRAPI endpoint URL and model version confirmed in writing
Task plan approved by rciapala
Any scope changes reflected in updated spec.md
risks: TRAPI auth mechanism unknown — blocks APP-01 and INF-03
Player scope ambiguity affects prompt template and validation thresholds
tasks.dependency_graph:
PM-01
  └── INF-01 (Provision dedicated storage + blob container)
        └── INF-02 (Provision Function App + Managed Identity)
              ├── INF-03 (Provision Key Vault + TRAPI secret)
              │     └── INF-04 (Managed Identity RBAC assignments)
              │           ├── APP-01 (TRAPI client + prompt template)
              │           │     └── APP-02 (Response validator)
              │           │           └── APP-03 (Blob writer with conditional PUT)
              │           │                 └── APP-04 (Timer Trigger function wiring)
              │           │                       └── APP-05 (host.json + local settings)
              │           │                             └── QA-01 (Unit tests)
              │           │                                   └── QA-02 (Integration tests)
              │           └── OBS-01 (Structured logging + custom metrics)
              │                 └── OBS-02 (Application Insights alert rules)
              └── REL-01 (GitHub Actions CI/CD workflow)
                    └── REL-02 (Staging slot smoke test)
                          └── REL-03 (Production slot swap)
                                └── DOC-01 (README documentation)

tasks.title: Provision dedicated storage account and blob container
tasks.description: Author or extend Bicep module in /infra to provision a dedicated Standard_LRS storage account (Cool access tier, allowSharedKeyAccess:false, soft-delete 7 days) with a private 'yankees-roster' container and no anonymous access. Separate from Function App host storage.
acceptance_criteria: Storage account provisioned with Standard_LRS SKU and Cool access tier
allowSharedKeyAccess set to false
Soft-delete enabled with 7-day retention on blob service
Container 'yankees-roster' exists with publicAccess: None
Storage account is distinct from Function App host storage account
Bicep deployment completes without errors in staging resource group
risks: Cool access tier may add latency for first read; acceptable for write-only nightly pattern
Existing storage.bicep may need refactoring to support two separate storage accounts

tasks.title: Provision Function App with system-assigned Managed Identity
tasks.description: Author or extend Bicep module in /infra to provision a Consumption Plan Linux Function App (Python 3.11) with system-assigned Managed Identity, APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING app setting, and AzureWebJobsStorage__accountName (identity-based host storage, no listKeys).
acceptance_criteria: Function App provisioned on Consumption Plan (kind: functionapp,linux, reserved: true)
linuxFxVersion set to Python|3.11
System-assigned Managed Identity enabled and object ID available
APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING app setting present
AzureWebJobsStorage__accountName set (no connection string with key)
Bicep deployment completes without errors
risks: Cold-start latency on Consumption Plan may occasionally push execution beyond 60s; 120s functionTimeout is the safety buffer

tasks.title: Provision Key Vault and store TRAPI credentials
tasks.description: Provision a zone-redundant Azure Key Vault in /infra Bicep. If TRAPI does not support Managed Identity, store the TRAPI API key/secret as a Key Vault secret and wire it as a Key Vault reference in the Function App app settings. Grant Function App Managed Identity 'Key Vault Secrets User' role.
acceptance_criteria: Key Vault provisioned with zone-redundant SKU
If TRAPI requires API key: secret stored in Key Vault and referenced via @Microsoft.KeyVault(...) in Function App app settings
Function App Managed Identity has 'Key Vault Secrets User' role assignment
No hardcoded credentials in Bicep or app settings
Key Vault soft-delete enabled
risks: TRAPI auth mechanism unknown until PM-01 resolves — Key Vault may be provisioned but unused if Managed Identity is supported

tasks.title: Configure Managed Identity RBAC role assignments
tasks.description: Extend /infra Bicep rbac module to assign 'Storage Blob Data Contributor' to the Function App Managed Identity scoped to the 'yankees-roster' container (not account-level), and 'Key Vault Secrets User' on the Key Vault. Also assign Blob Data Owner + Queue Data Contributor on host storage.
acceptance_criteria: 'Storage Blob Data Contributor' assigned to Managed Identity scoped to 'yankees-roster' container (not account-level)
'Key Vault Secrets User' assigned to Managed Identity on the Key Vault
Blob Data Owner + Queue Data Contributor assigned on host storage account
Role assignments verified via Azure CLI: az role assignment list
No overly broad account-level storage assignments present
risks: Container-scoped RBAC requires resource ID of the specific container; Bicep resource hierarchy must be correct

tasks.title: Scaffold GitHub Actions CI/CD workflow with staging slot swap
tasks.description: Create a GitHub Actions workflow (.github/workflows/cd.yml) that on push to main: installs Python deps, runs tests, deploys to a staging slot, executes a smoke test, and swaps staging to production. Use azure/functions-action with slot support.
acceptance_criteria: Workflow triggers on push to main branch
pip install -r requirements.txt runs successfully in CI
pytest runs and fails the workflow on test failure
Deployment targets staging slot before production
Smoke test step verifies staging slot is healthy before swap
Slot swap to production occurs only after smoke test passes
No secrets hardcoded in workflow YAML; GitHub Secrets used for credentials
risks: Staging slot requires Standard or Premium plan for slot support on Function Apps; Consumption Plan does not support deployment slots — may need to use a separate staging Function App instead

tasks.title: Implement TRAPI client with retry/backoff and prompt template
tasks.description: Implement /app/trapi_client.py: loads prompt from prompts/get_1985_yankees.txt, acquires Managed Identity bearer token via DefaultAzureCredential for the TRAPI scope, sends POST to TRAPI GPT-4o endpoint with 45-second timeout and 3 exponential-backoff retries. Pin the GPT-4o model version in the prompt or config.
inputs: TRAPI endpoint + auth scope from PM-01
acceptance_criteria: Prompt template loaded from prompts/get_1985_yankees.txt at runtime (not hardcoded)
GPT-4o model version pinned in prompt template or TRAPI request body
Bearer token acquired via DefaultAzureCredential with correct TRAPI scope
HTTP timeout set to 45 seconds
3 retries with exponential backoff on transient HTTP errors (429, 5xx)
TRAPI response body returned as parsed JSON
Module-level credential reuse (no new credential per call)
risks: TRAPI scope value unknown until PM-01 resolves; placeholder must be replaced
Exponential backoff may push total execution time beyond 60s on retries — acceptable given 120s functionTimeout

tasks.title: Implement response validator for roster schema and player count
tasks.description: Implement /app/validator.py: validates TRAPI response against expected JSON schema (players array with name/position/jersey_number fields) and checks player count is in range 24–28. Returns validated roster on success or raises a ValidationError on failure.
acceptance_criteria: Validates presence of 'players' array in response
Each player object validated for 'name' (string), 'position' (string), 'jersey_number' (integer) fields
Player count outside 24–28 range raises ValidationError
ValidationError includes raw response payload for failed-path blob write
Validation passes for responses containing Mattingly, Winfield, Henderson
Unit tests cover: valid response, missing fields, count too low, count too high
risks: Player scope ambiguity (active roster only vs. broader) affects valid count range — defer to PM-01 resolution

tasks.title: Implement blob writer with conditional PUT and failed-path handling
tasks.description: Implement /app/blob_writer.py: writes validated roster JSON to 'yankees-roster/{run_date_utc}.json' using BlobClient with DefaultAzureCredential. Uses If-None-Match: * (conditional PUT) to prevent duplicate writes. On ValidationError, writes raw response to 'yankees-roster/failed/{run_date_utc}.json'.
acceptance_criteria: BlobClient authenticates via DefaultAzureCredential (no connection string with key)
Blob name is 'yankees-roster/{run_date_utc}.json' where run_date_utc is UTC date string
Upload uses If-None-Match: * header; ResourceExistsError is caught and logged (not re-raised)
Failed-path blob written to 'yankees-roster/failed/{run_date_utc}.json' on ValidationError
Storage account URL sourced from environment variable (not hardcoded)
Unit tests verify conditional PUT logic and failed-path write
risks: Cool access tier adds negligible write latency; no risk for this pattern
Container-scoped RBAC must be verified before integration test

tasks.title: Implement Timer Trigger function and wire dependencies
tasks.description: Implement /app/function_app.py using Azure Functions v2 SDK: define GetAndStoreYankeesRoster with @app.timer_trigger(schedule='0 0 2 * * *', use_monitor=True). Wire TRAPI client, validator, and blob writer. Emit structured log events at each key step. Handle ValidationError by writing to failed/ path and logging.
acceptance_criteria: Timer trigger schedule is '0 0 2 * * *' (6-part CRON for Azure Functions)
use_monitor=True set on trigger decorator
Function calls TRAPI client, validates response, writes blob in sequence
ValidationError caught: failed-path blob written, alert custom metric emitted, function exits without re-raise (no infinite retry)
Structured log events emitted: function_started, trapi_request_sent, trapi_response_received, blob_write_succeeded, function_completed
maxConcurrentCalls = 1 configured
risks: 6-part CRON syntax (with seconds) required by Azure Functions — ensure '0 0 2 * * *' not '0 2 * * *'

tasks.title: Configure host.json, requirements, and local dev settings
tasks.description: Update host.json with functionTimeout=120s, extensionBundle, and logging/sampling settings. Update requirements-prod.txt with runtime dependencies (azure-functions, azure-storage-blob, azure-identity, opentelemetry). Provide local.settings.json.example with all required env vars documented.
acceptance_criteria: host.json functionTimeout set to '00:02:00' (120 seconds)
Application Insights sampling enabled in host.json
maxConcurrentCalls: 1 present in extensions config
requirements-prod.txt includes azure-functions>=1.17.0, azure-storage-blob>=12.19.0, azure-identity, opentelemetry-sdk
local.settings.json.example documents all required env vars (TRAPI_ENDPOINT, TRAPI_SCOPE, STORAGE_ACCOUNT_URL, etc.)
local.settings.json is in .gitignore

tasks.title: Write unit tests for TRAPI client, validator, and blob writer
tasks.description: Write pytest unit tests in /tests covering: TRAPI client retry/backoff and timeout, response validator schema and count validation, blob writer conditional PUT and failed-path logic. Use mocks for Azure SDK and HTTP calls.
acceptance_criteria: pytest runs without errors: python -m pytest tests/ -v --cov=app --cov-report=term-missing
Coverage >80% on trapi_client, validator, and blob_writer modules
Test asserts known players (Mattingly, Winfield, Henderson) pass validation
Test asserts player count <24 raises ValidationError
Test asserts player count >28 raises ValidationError
Test asserts conditional PUT raises no error on ResourceExistsError (idempotent)
Test asserts failed-path blob written on ValidationError
risks: Azure SDK mocking complexity — use unittest.mock or pytest-mock consistently

tasks.title: Implement structured logging and custom metrics via OpenTelemetry
tasks.description: Integrate OpenTelemetry SDK in function_app.py to emit structured log events (function_started, trapi_request_sent with model+prompt_hash, trapi_response_received with token_count+latency+player_count, blob_write_succeeded with blob URI, function_completed) and emit player_count_returned custom metric to Application Insights.
acceptance_criteria: All five key log events emitted with required fields on each execution
player_count_returned custom metric emitted as a numeric value to Application Insights
prompt_hash included in trapi_request_sent log (SHA-256 of prompt content)
Log events queryable in Log Analytics workspace via KQL
No PII or secrets logged
Application Insights sampling configured (not 100%) to control ingestion cost
risks: OpenTelemetry + Azure Functions integration requires correct OTEL exporter config; test in staging before production

tasks.title: Provision Application Insights alert rules for failure, duration, and data quality
tasks.description: Extend /infra Bicep alerts module to add: (1) execution failure alert (failure count > 0 in 1-hour window), (2) duration alert (execution duration > 90 seconds), (3) data quality alert (player_count_returned < 24 or > 40). All alerts notify rciapala via email Action Group.
acceptance_criteria: Execution failure alert: Scheduled Query Rule fires when exceptions > 0 in 1-hour window
Duration alert: fires when function duration metric > 90000ms
Data quality alert: fires when player_count_returned custom metric < 24 or > 40
All three alerts linked to email Action Group for rciapala
Alert rules deployed via Bicep without errors
Test alert fire verified by manually triggering condition in staging
risks: Custom metric alert requires player_count_returned to be emitted correctly by OBS-01 before alert can be validated

tasks.title: Security hardening: secret scan, network review, and access model validation
tasks.description: Run a secret scan (e.g., truffleHog or git-secrets) on the repository to confirm no credentials committed. Verify all app settings use Key Vault references or environment variables. Confirm TRAPI is accessible without VNet (or document VNet requirements). Review Managed Identity role assignments for least privilege.
outputs: Security review checklist (documented in PR or ADR)
acceptance_criteria: Secret scan passes with zero findings on main branch
No connection strings with keys in app settings or source code
All sensitive config uses Key Vault references or Managed Identity
RBAC assignments are least-privilege (container-scoped, not account-level)
Blob container has no anonymous access (publicAccess: None confirmed)
Network access to TRAPI documented: public or VNet-restricted
risks: If TRAPI requires VNet, this task expands significantly and blocks production deployment

tasks.title: Write integration test verifying known players in blob output
tasks.description: Write an integration test in /tests that invokes the function end-to-end against staging infrastructure, reads the output blob, and asserts Don Mattingly, Dave Winfield, and Rickey Henderson are present with valid position and jersey_number fields.
acceptance_criteria: Integration test connects to staging storage account via Managed Identity or test credential
Test triggers function execution (or reads most recent blob if run post-execution)
Blob content parsed and validated against expected schema
Mattingly, Winfield, Henderson all present with non-null position and integer jersey_number
Test fails clearly if blob does not exist or player is missing
Integration test is excluded from standard unit test run (marked with pytest.mark.integration)
risks: Integration test requires live Azure resources (staging environment must be deployed first)

tasks.title: Execute staging deployment and end-to-end smoke test
tasks.description: Deploy the function to the staging environment via GitHub Actions, manually trigger or wait for the nightly execution, verify the output blob is written correctly, confirm all alert rules are active, and run the integration test suite.
acceptance_criteria: Function deploys to staging without errors via GitHub Actions
Manual trigger (or nightly execution) produces output blob at 'yankees-roster/{date}.json'
Integration test passes: Mattingly, Winfield, Henderson present in blob
All three alert rules verified active in Application Insights
Structured log events visible in Log Analytics
No errors in Application Insights during staging execution
Go/no-go decision documented
risks: Consumption Plan cold-start may delay first execution; wait up to 5 minutes after trigger
Staging slot swap caveat: Consumption Plan may require separate staging Function App

tasks.title: Execute production slot swap and verify nightly execution
tasks.description: Swap staging to production via GitHub Actions workflow after smoke test approval. Monitor the first production nightly execution at 2:00 AM UTC, verify blob output, and confirm all alerts remain in healthy state.
acceptance_criteria: Production Function App running latest code after slot swap
First nightly execution at 2:00 AM UTC produces blob at 'yankees-roster/{date}.json'
No execution failures in Application Insights within first 24 hours
Duration alert not triggered (execution < 90s)
player_count_returned metric in range 24–28
Deployment documented in release notes or PR
risks: First production execution window is 2 AM UTC — ensure oncall monitoring is active
If execution fails silently, useMonitor:true surfaces it in App Insights

tasks.title: Complete README with local dev setup, TRAPI auth, and blob naming
tasks.description: Update README.md to include: local development setup (Python venv, local.settings.json.example usage, Azurite for local storage), TRAPI auth instructions (how to obtain scope and configure Managed Identity or Key Vault fallback), blob naming convention, and how to run unit and integration tests.
acceptance_criteria: README includes step-by-step local dev setup instructions
TRAPI auth instructions explain Managed Identity scope and Key Vault fallback
Blob naming convention documented with example: 'yankees-roster/2026-03-31.json'
Instructions for running unit tests: python -m pytest tests/ -v
Instructions for running integration tests with pytest.mark.integration
README reviewed and approved by rciapala
